katex.title: Formulas (KaTeX)
katex.summary: Write LaTeX formulas inline ($…$) or centered ($$…$$).

mermaid.title: Diagrams (Mermaid)
mermaid.summary: Flowcharts, sequences, Gantt charts… described as text in a code block.

geogebra.title: GeoGebra
geogebra.summary: Embed an activity published on geogebra.org into a course.

jsxgraph.title: Figures (JSXGraph)
jsxgraph.summary: Plot interactive curves and points from equations.

tikz.title: Figures (TikZ)
tikz.summary: Draw precise vector diagrams using LaTeX/TikZ syntax.

katex.intro: Mathematical formulas are written in LaTeX and rendered by KaTeX, in any text or exercise block. Every example below is editable: use it as a starting point.

delimiters.title: Delimiters
delimiters.body: An inline formula goes between $…$, a centered one between $$…$$. A few rules keep prices from turning into formulas:
delimiters.ruleNoSpace: no space right after the opening $ nor right before the closing $;
delimiters.ruleDigit: a closing $ followed by a digit stays plain text (“10$ and 20$”);
delimiters.ruleBlockLine: a $$…$$ block must close at the end of a line (multi-line content allowed);

basics.title: Fractions, powers, roots
basics.body: \frac{a}{b} for a fraction (\dfrac forces display size inline), ^ for exponents, _ for subscripts — braces required beyond one character —, \sqrt{x} and \sqrt[n]{x} for roots.

operators.title: Sums, integrals, limits
operators.body: Big operators take their bounds as sub/superscripts: \sum, \int, \lim, \prod. \, inserts a thin space (before dx for instance).

symbols.title: Greek letters and symbols
symbols.body: Greek letters are \alpha, \pi, \Omega…; comparisons \le \ge \ne \approx; operations \times \cdot \pm; sets \mathbb{R}, \in, \subset, \cup, \cap; arrows \rightarrow, \implies, \iff.

matrices.title: Matrices
matrices.body: pmatrix (parentheses), bmatrix (brackets) and vmatrix (determinant) environments: & separates columns, \\ separates rows.

systems.title: Systems and alignment
systems.body: cases for piecewise definitions, aligned to line up several equalities on the = sign (marked with &).

text.title: Text inside a formula
text.body: \text{…} inserts upright text inside a formula; \, \quad and \qquad control spacing.

errors.title: Invalid formulas
errors.body: An invalid formula never breaks the block: it renders in red with its source visible until you fix it.

mermaid.intro: A “mermaid” code block describes a diagram as text; it renders automatically (the source stays visible until rendering runs). The first two examples are editable.

flowchart.title: Flowchart
flowchart.body: graph TD (top-down) or graph LR (left-right). Nodes: [rectangle], {decision diamond}, (rounded). Arrows --> take a label between pipes: -->|yes|.

sequence.title: Sequence diagram
sequence.body: sequenceDiagram describes exchanges: participant declares an actor (with an alias), ->> a solid arrow, -->> a dashed reply.

gallery.title: Other diagram types
gallery.body: Mermaid covers many more needs — each source below can be copied into a mermaid block:

theme.title: Theme
theme.body: Diagrams automatically follow the app's light/dark mode — nothing to configure; do not hardcode colors in the source.

math.title: Limitation: no LaTeX inside nodes
math.body: $…$ formulas are not rendered inside a mermaid node (a note under the diagram reminds you). Write the formula in the text around the diagram.

errors.title: Invalid diagrams
errors.body: An invalid source does not break the rest of the block: the source is kept with the error message as a caption.

geogebra.intro: A “geogebra” code block embeds a GeoGebra activity published on geogebra.org, displayed in an interactive frame.

findId.title: Finding an activity's identifier
findId.body: Open the activity on geogebra.org: the identifier is the code at the end of the address. That code alone is what you provide — never the full address.

keys.title: Block keys
keys.body: One key per line, in key=value format:
keys.id: material identifier (required, letters and digits only);
keys.height: height in pixels (default 450, bounds 150–900). Non-numeric value → default, out of bounds → clamped.

invalid.title: Invalid identifier
invalid.body: An identifier that is not strictly alphanumeric (a URL, for instance) never renders a frame: a notice points it out.

print.title: Printing and PDF export
print.body: A GeoGebra activity is interactive and cannot be printed: in the PDF export it is replaced by the note “Interactive content: see the online version”.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.body: Content is loaded from geogebra.org (external, sandboxed frame): students viewing the course load a third-party resource. Worth factoring into your school's data policy.

jsxgraph.intro: A “jsxgraph” code block draws a mathematical figure (curves, points) rendered locally — no external service —, interactive (pan, zoom) and printable.

first.title: First plot
first.body: equation= plots the curve of a function of x. Edit the equation below and watch the plot.

syntax.title: Equation syntax
syntax.functions: usual functions: sin, cos, tan, exp, log, sqrt, abs.

multiple.title: Several curves and points
multiple.body: Repeat equation= to overlay curves, and point=x,y to mark points (here the intersections).

bbox.title: Framing
bbox.body: bbox=xmin,ymax,xmax,ymin sets the visible area (default -5,5,5,-5). Mind the order: top-left corner then bottom-right corner.

errors.title: Invalid input
errors.body: A malformed line is ignored (the figure draws what is valid); an unparsable equation shows a notice instead of the figure.

print.title: Printing and PDF export
print.body: The figure is drawn as SVG: it goes into the course PDF export as-is.

tikz.intro: A “tikz” code block compiles LaTeX/TikZ code into an SVG directly in the browser — no external service —, crisp vector rendering and natively printable.

first.title: First drawing
first.body: The block's content is the body of a tikzpicture environment (added automatically if missing). Edit the drawing below.

advanced.title: TikZ options
advanced.body: The usual tikzpicture options (scale, fill colors…) are available by writing out the environment with its brackets.

print.title: Printing and PDF export
print.body: The rendering is a vector SVG: it goes into the course PDF export as-is, with crisp text and lines.

resourcePicker.title: Insert a resource
resourcePicker.empty: No resource available. Add some from the Resources tab.

markdownHelp.title: Formatting help
markdownHelp.intro: The editor combines three languages: Markdown for text, LaTeX for formulas and Mermaid for diagrams.

markdown.title: Markdown
markdown.heading: Headings, from largest to smallest
markdown.table: Table: a header row, a separator row, then the data rows.
markdown.resourceNote: To insert a library file (image, audio, video, document), use the “Insert a resource” button below the editor: the link stays valid over time, unlike a URL pasted by hand.

latex.title: Formulas (LaTeX)
latex.intro: Rendered by KaTeX. An invalid formula shows in red without breaking the rest of the block.
latex.block: Centered formula, on its own line

mermaid.title: Diagrams (Mermaid)
mermaid.intro: Describe a diagram in a code block tagged “mermaid”; it is rendered automatically in the preview.
mermaid.types: Mermaid also supports sequence diagrams, Gantt charts, pie charts and many more.
mermaid.mathNote: LaTeX (`$…$`) is not rendered inside Mermaid nodes: put your formulas in the text, around the diagram.

geogebra.title: GeoGebra activities
geogebra.intro: Embeds an activity published on geogebra.org: provide its identifier in a “geogebra” code block.

jsxgraph.title: Dynamic figures (JSXGraph)
jsxgraph.intro: Plots interactive curves and points from equations, in a “jsxgraph” code block.
